MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT FRANCHISE TAX BOARD'S, AN AGENCY OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA, MOTION FOR LEAVE TO FILE COMPULSORY CROSS-COMPLAINT.
APPEARANCES: (A.M. APPEARANCES) MARTIN SCHAINBAUM, ESQUIRE AND STEPHEN DeROSA, ESQUIRE (LAW OFFICES OF MARTIN A. SCHAINBAUM, 415.777.1040) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. THERE ARE NO OTHER APPEARANCES. (P.M. APPEARANCES) AS ABOVE AND INCLUDE THE FOLLOWING: JEFFREY A. RICH, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL (OFFICE OF THE ATTORNEY GENERAL, 916.324.5154) AND MICHAEL CORNEZ, TAX COUNSEL IV (CALIFORNIA FRANCHISE TAX BOARD, 916.845.5305) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. PLAINTIFF HAS NOT DEMONSTRATED THAT DEFENDANT ENGAGED IN BAD FAITH. PROBATE CODE SECTION 9201 GOVERNS CLAIMS BY PUBLIC ENTITIES AND SPECIFIES THAT REVENUE TAX CODE SECTION 19517 APPLIES TO THE TIMELINESS OF CLAIMS. DEFENDANT'S CLAIM IS TIMELY. PLAINTIFF'S OBJECTIONS, BASED ON THE MERITS, MAY BE RESOLVED IN THE COURSE OF LITIGATION. MR. CORNEZ IS TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
